**Clinical Reasoning:** While the retrieved evidence discusses various eye diseases, genetic mechanisms (such as gene therapy, miRNA dysregulation, and specific gene mutations), and emerging therapies involving siRNA and antisense oligonucleotides, there is **no mention of circular RNAs** or any related molecular pathway involved in ocular pathology. All referenced non-coding RNAs discussed are either microRNAs (as in Vogt-Koyanagi-Harada syndrome) or short-interfering RNAs used therapeutically. Therefore, based solely on available data, circular RNAs are not implicated in known eye diseases.
